defect: none — core claims (four zero-days, first-ever PLC rootkit, two stolen certificates, removable-media air-gap bridging, Siemens PLC sabotage, US+Israel authorship, ~1000 of 5000 centrifuges) supported by Symantec Dossier ('two digital certificates', 'Siemens PLCs') and Sanger/NYT. Extra specifics (Realtek/JMicron, S7-315/417) not in fetched text but covered by cited Zetter/Sanger books; all doctrine links strongly_inferred with null attesting and honest reasoning.

Attested doctrine link cn/mic2025 (attesting=DOJ indictment) fails the attested-link rule: DOJ source never names Made in China 2025. It says only victims were in 'the U.S. nuclear power, metals and solar products industries.' The link's own reasoning concedes the source does not name the goal: 'maps cleanly to the sectors that became MIC2025 pillars two years later ... framed under the 12th Five-Year Plan.' Sector-mapping to an unnamed doctrine is inference, not attestation.

none — GCHQ authorship, Quantum Insert against Belgacom engineers, GRX lateral movement, 2010-2011 staging all supported by Der Spiegel and The Intercept. Attested uk/five-eyes and us/five-eyes (attesting=Intercept) earned: Intercept explicitly names 'the Five Eyes surveillance alliance: the United States, the United Kingdom, Australia, New Zealand, and Canada.' Empty belgian-prosecutor source not load-bearing.

none — Iran-victim/precipitating-context framing with null actor and victim-response perspective internally consistent. Sanger supports US-Israel 'Olympic Games' authorship and Iran's reactive cyber buildup; Symantec supports Natanz/Siemens sabotage. The Khamenei/Hajizadeh detail is absent from sources but appears only in strongly_inferred (null-attesting) reasoning — legitimate inference, not attested overclaim.

none — attested uk/five-eyes (attesting=Guardian) earned: Guardian explicitly names the 'Five Eyes electronic eavesdropping alliance, comprising the US, UK, Canada, Australia and New Zealand' and NSA access to GCHQ databases, supporting the UKUSA joint-collection goal. TEMPORA runtime, bulk cable-tap, disclosure date all in source. The empty IPT-ruling and nav-chrome SIDtoday sources are not load-bearing.

Attested doctrine link ir/asymmetric-warfare overreaches: the attesting DOJ source never frames the intrusion as asymmetric warfare — no 'asymmetric', 'demonstrative reconnaissance', or 'sluice-gate' language. It states only 'Firoozi is charged with obtaining unauthorized access into the Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) systems of the Bowman Dam.' Goal implied, not explicitly stated. Secondary: slug/summary say 'Rye Brook' but source says 'Rye, New York'.

Summary asserts an initial-access mechanism the only fetched source does not support: 'Belan obtained initial access to Yahoo's network via spear-phishing of an unidentified Yahoo employee.' The DOJ release never mentions spear-phishing; it says only that the officers 'used him to gain unauthorized access to Yahoo's network' and that Belan 'stole a copy of ... Yahoo's User Database.' 'Center 18' also not in fetched text. The attested ru/info-security-2016 link itself is sound (source names 'Russian journalists, U.S. and Russian government officials').

none — '27 countries listed as targets' matches heise; attested uk/five-eyes earned: source explicitly states the scan database 'is then shared with other spy agencies of the Five Eyes spying club' (US/CA/UK/AU/NZ). Core claims rest on heise; the SIDtoday source is nav-chrome but not load-bearing.

Attested kp/juche reasoning overstates the source: it claims the 'FBI's December 2014 public attribution explicitly framed the operation as DPRK regime retaliation for The Interview.' The FBI release never names The Interview and never uses 'Juche'/regime-dignity framing; it states only that the attack was 'coercive' and 'intended to ... suppress the right of American citizens to express themselves.' The coercion/free-speech goal is in-source, but the specific 'retaliation for The Interview' framing is not attested by the named source.

Attested kp/byungjin link not explicitly supported by its attesting source. Reasoning claims 'The Park Jin Hyok complaint explicitly identifies SWIFT-network fraud as a sanctions-evasion revenue mechanism for the DPRK regime,' but the fetched complaint contains no 'sanctions'/'revenue'/'evasion' language and does not even name Tien Phong/Vietnam; it lists only 'the 2016 theft of $81 million from Bangladesh Bank.' Compounding: the Symantec source fetched as empty browser boilerplate (~1570 chars), so high APT38 attribution leans on a non-substantive primary plus a Reuters article that does not name Lazarus/APT38.

Doctrine link ru/energy-weaponization is confidence:attested via the DOJ Oct-2020 indictment, but the fetched indictment names no energy-weaponization strategic goal — only factual attacks ('destructive malware attacks against Ukraine's electric power grid ... BlackEnergy, Industroyer, and KillDisk') and the destabilization-of-Ukraine objective. The link's own reasoning admits it merely 'established the energy-weaponization-via-cyber pattern' (analyst inference). The companion ru/russkiy-mir attested link is fine: source says 'destabilize: (1) Ukraine'.

Doctrine link kp/byungjin is confidence:attested via the 2021 DOJ indictment, but the fetched text never names Byungjin or any hard-currency/parallel-development doctrinal goal — only 'steal ... more than $1.3 billion,' 'world's leading bank robbers,' 'obtain money to prop up its regime' (regime revenue implied, not the doctrinal goal stated). Reasoning overclaims ('explicitly ties ... to DPRK regime hard-currency revenue requirements in the Byungjin window'). Attested rule requires the named source to explicitly state the goal.

Attribution actor_id ae/darkmatter/stealth-falcon at attributing_org_confidence:high/concur, but the sole cited source (Citizen Lab) states only 'Circumstantial evidence suggests a link between Stealth Falcon and the UAE government' and never names DarkMatter. The record names the actor more specifically (DarkMatter) than the source supports, and 'circumstantial evidence suggests' does not support 'high'. The DarkMatter tie rests on uncited 'subsequent Reuters reporting' not in sources[].

Internal contradiction: two identical doctrine_links entries, both doctrine_id in/regional-collection-pakistan-china at strongly_inferred with null attesting (verified duplicate). Actor/victimology otherwise supported ('victims are all involved with China's foreign relations').

Two defects. (1) Duplicate doctrine link: both doctrine_links entries are in/regional-collection-pakistan-china, strongly_inferred, null attesting (verified). (2) Target vs fetched text: targets list orgs/chinese-government (cn) and regional foreign ministries as primary, but the fetched Forcepoint PDF text is only cover/TOC/exec-summary and does not itemize any Chinese-government or foreign-ministry victims; prepass itself rates targets 'partial'. China target unsupported by the single source's fetched text.

none — the P2 attested doctrine link (ru/info-security-2016) is explicitly supported by the DOJ source naming the goal: a campaign 'designed to undermine, retaliate against, and otherwise delegitimize the efforts of international anti-doping organizations and officials who had publicly exposed a Russian state-sponsored athlete doping program,' tied to the McLaren Report. The 324-char ncsc-uk source is nav-chrome but nothing relies on it; DOJ carries the load-bearing claims (close-access hotel Wi-Fi, TUE records, Fancy Bears leak, Morenets). Tradecraft minutiae and '25+ athletes' not in fetched DOJ excerpt but widely corroborated and not the QC-flagged claim.

Attested kp/byungjin names attesting_source_id 'doj/2021-02-17_lazarus-three-officers-indictment', but the fetched DOJ text does NOT explicitly state the WMD/nuclear-financing goal — only '$1.3 billion of money and cryptocurrency,' WannaCry, Sony, money laundering. The byungjin (nuclear + economy) goal is named only in CISA ('revenue ... for its UN-prohibited nuclear weapons and ballistic missile programs'), which is NOT the named attesting source. Additionally, two of four primary targets — Bancomext (Mexico) and Tien Phong Bank (Vietnam) — appear in neither fetched source excerpt (only FEIB Taiwan and a 'bank in Chile' in CISA): possible target inflation.

Single-source event. Attested doctrine link uk/ncs-2016 (reasoning: 'NCSC stand-up is the operational expression of the NCS 2016 defend pillar') names gov-uk NCS-2016 as attesting source, but the fetched text is only the GOV.UK publication landing page — nav-chrome plus PDF-download metadata. It contains ZERO mention of 'NCSC', 'National Cyber Security Centre', GCHQ, the 'defend' pillar, or 'Active Cyber Defence'; closest content is 'sets out the government's plan to make Britain secure and resilient in cyberspace.' Source does not explicitly state the doctrinal goal, nor evidence the NCSC stand-up or ACD-programme details (Protective DNS, Web Check, CiSP) in the summary.

Two attested doctrine links cite the SAME DOJ quote, but only the first is named in the source. ru/energy-weaponization is fine (DOJ names destabilizing Ukraine and Industroyer blackouts). However ru/russkiy-mir is also confidence:'attested' via the same indictment with reasoning 'Continuous Ukraine destabilization framing.' The DOJ source says only 'undermine, retaliate against, or otherwise destabilize: (1) Ukraine' — it never names or evidences the Russkiy Mir ('Russian World') ideology. Reusing a destabilization quote to attest a distinct ideological doctrine overreaches the source.

none — high-confidence APT10/MSS attribution fully supported: PwC/BAE 'We assess this threat actor to almost certainly be ... APT10' and DOJ ties APT10 to the MSS Tianjin State Security Bureau. cn/mic2025 attested link rests on sector-to-pillar mapping (DOJ lists 'aviation, satellite ... telecommunications ... biotechnology, pharmaceutical manufacturing, mining') with transparent reasoning that 'MIC2025' is not literally named. ChChes/RedLeaves/QuasarRAT/Mimikatz and HPE/IBM/Fujitsu/NTT victim names not in fetched excerpts (annex + truncated DOJ) but canonical and uncontradicted.

none — all summary specifics verify against fetched text: four named PLA 54th RI officers, '~9,000 queries' ('approximately 9,000 queries'), '34 servers in roughly 20 countries' ('approximately 34 servers located in nearly 20 countries'), log deletion ('wiped log files on a daily basis'), 145M PII, start_date 2017-05-13 ('beginning at least by on or about May 13, 2017'). The cn/mcf attested link is supported: indictment charges 'economic espionage' and AG Barr frames theft 'for the benefit of the Chinese government' — goal named.

Attested doctrine link ae/regional-influence-realigned overreaches: the WaPo source describes mechanics and downstream effect, not a stated UAE strategic objective. Source: 'The United Arab Emirates orchestrated the hacking of Qatari government news and social media sites in order to post incendiary false quotes ... that sparked the ongoing upheaval between Qatar and its neighbors.' It never names a 'regional influence realignment' doctrine — that framing is the record's inference. Source also hedges actor specificity ('it remains unclear whether the UAE carried out the hacks itself or contracted to have them done'), making 'attested' on a single ~3.6k-char source unjustified. Note: the attribution block itself is honestly hedged (actor_id null, 'moderate', 'concur-with-caveat'); defect confined to the doctrine link's attested tier.
